name: Soft Biscotti
recipeInstructions:
1. Sift together flour, sugar, salt, baking powder, and baking soda.
Add the butter and mix until a pie dough forms.
2. Make a well in the center of the mixture and add the eggs. Stir in
the vanilla extract. Knead the dough to form a medium soft ball.
Gradually add milk, working it into the dough until it becomes
smooth and pliable (if too soft, add flour 2 tablespoons at a time).
3. Roll out the dough on a lightly floured surface to form a rope about
18 inches long and 1/2 inch thick. Place the dough evenly on a
baking sheet.
4. Bake in a preheated oven at 325°F for 45 to 50 minutes, or until
the loaves are just turning pale pink.
5. Remove from the oven and let cool slightly before cutting into
slices. Transfer the cut slices to an unheated broiler pan and toast
under low heat until lightly browned on both sides.
NOTE: If you prefer a softer texture, toast for less time; for a firmer
texture, toast longer. Store in an airtight container for up to several weeks.
